rationale_for_baseline_tier: An afebrile scalp psoriasis flare with excoriation but no honey crusts/pus/spreading warmth is not infected — low acuity, managed with stopping harsh shampoos, a topical steroid scalp lotion and gentle care. The only escalation is for secondary infection.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Redness spreading down the forehead/face, thick yellow pus, hot hard skin, or fever
escalation_edges.rationale: Indicates secondary bacterial infection needing antibiotics.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the redness spreads down your face, thick yellow pus appears, the skin becomes hot and hard, or you get a fever, see a doctor the same day.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Reassure explicitly that this is not an infection and hair loss is not expected.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is a scalp psoriasis flare from the harsh shampoo, not an infection, and your hair won't fall out from it. Stop the harsh shampoos, apply the steroid scalp lotion and leave your hair unwashed for about a day, keep your nails short, and use gentle cleansers. See a doctor the same day if the redness spreads down your face, thick pus appears, or you get a fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP within 7-10 days; same-day if infection signs
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: No after-hours care needed unless infection signs develop.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth impression of a scalp psoriasis flare with excoriation; impetigo/cellulitis/zoster excluded (silvery scale, afebrile, no honey crusts, bilateral); harsh shampoos stopped, topical steroid scalp lotion advised; secondary-infection safety-net given.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring without an infection screen or safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A secondary impetiginisation/cellulitis of excoriated skin is missed and progresses.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Antibiotics/ED for an uninfected excoriated flare is over-triage.
